meta.description: Normalised Difference Water Index.   The satellite images captured at low tide (bottom 30% of the springs tidal range) are used to compute a composite NDWI (Normalized Difference Water Index) image. The 70th percentile is taken when calculating the composite to give more weight to dry pixels.

This layer shows a time series of the Low Tide line from 2022 to 2025.  The contour was derived from all the low-tide cloud-free Landsat and Sentinel-2 images available.

For each section of contour, the data available in the layer comprises:
* *id*: unique identifier of the line
* *date*: date of the low tide event
* *springs_tide_range*: the mean springs tidal range at this location according to the FES2022 tidal model
* *obs_tide_level*: observed tide level across the satellite images used (average)
* *images_in_composite*: number of satellite images used to create the composite for this line

Select the data values tab and click on a feature to view details
Low water line data derived from Landsat and Sentinel-2 optical images coupled with tidal model data.

Global land cover map at 10 m resolution derived from Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 observations, produced within the ESA WorldCover project. It is useful in urban contexts to understand how land is distributed between natural areas, agriculture, and built-up zones, for land-use planning and monitoring of urban expansion into surrounding landscapes.

attribution.text: ESA WorldCover project / Copernicus Sentinel data

Global maps of temporary crops for 2021, at 10m resolution. This identifies pieces of land that are sowed/planted and harvestable at least once within the 12 months after the sowing/planting date.
AI-detected conservation and conventional tillage events at parcel level across Estonia (2018–2022). Parcels rasterised at 10 m resolution. Derived from Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2 and Landsat imagery.
Annual (2017-2023) pan-European mapping of secondary crops at a spatial resolution of 10 m and a MMU of 0.25 ha.
Annual (2017-2023) pan-European level amapping of the presence of fallow land over cropland, at a spatial resolution of 10 m and a MMU of 0.25 ha

Normalized Difference Vegetation Index, derived from Sentinel-2 optical reflectance using red and near-infrared bands. It is updated daily to capture vegetation greenness and photosynthetic activity. It supports urban environmental monitoring by detecting vegetation stress, seasonal dynamics, and vegetation gradients.

Leaf Area Index derived from Sentinel-2 observations using radiative transfer modelling. It estimates vegetation canopy density as leaf area per unit ground surface. It characterizes canopy structure, density, and seasonal variability.
